notes: Smallest round-trip for antigravity: one user prompt, one terminal text-only reply, no tool use, asserting the ready→working→ready arc. Single-birth adapter (transcript-first: SessionIDFromPath derives the session id from the <conv-id> directory, so the session is born directly in ready when transcript.jsonl appears — NO transient proc-<PID> presession row, like claudecode/kiro-cli, unlike codex). parseUserInput emits user_message→working; the turn's terminal MODEL/PLANNER_RESPONSE with no tool_calls is reclassified turn_done→ready (parser.go ~L113-118). turn_end anchors to turn_start so the matcher picks the post-turn ready, not the birth ready. Tokens/cost are NOT in the antigravity JSONL (per-conversation SQLite protobuf store the parser does not read), so the scenario's TotalTokens>0 criterion is not observable — observations assert tokens_nonzero:false, cost_nonzero:false (same as the sibling kiro-cli cell). Model IS observable (harvested from <USER_SETTINGS_CHANGE>, 'Gemini 3.5 Flash' in the live samples) but the exact normalized string is provisional, so model is omitted here for record to settle. Recorded with the INTERACTIVE driver (agy under tmux): headless agy --print exits at turn completion, before the daemon can observe the terminal working→ready edge. driver=gap:wait_turn — record ports the wait_turn turn-done poll + the launch_repl session-id/transcript-path SEAM before driving.

text: antigravity session (the <conv-id>) appears in ready when transcript.jsonl is written (single-birth, no proc-<PID> presession)
User prompt (USER_INPUT) → working on the <conv-id> session
Terminal MODEL/PLANNER_RESPONSE with no tool_calls → turn_done → back to ready; state never entered waiting
